reviews.review_msg: This is my second class with Newton (ASC201 in 2014). As a senior, I believe the class is a failure. Newton's lectures are void of analysis (bynd claiming PRC protects minorities LOL), and what is more troubling, at best inaccurate. Frequent errors, zero citations, errors in test design. No critical thought taught, rather repetition demanded. Stop.

You can tell he really cares about what he's teaching. He has a very calm presence and is genuinely interested by his student's reflections, which is why there's quite a bit of homework. I found the material really interesting and inspiring, and the tests were easy if you read the textbook

Literally the worst class I've taken so far (4th yr student with a 3.4gpa, not amazing but I'm not an idiot). Be prepared to read 100 pages of religious texts/philosophy etc. Weekly quizzes will be extremely specific and based only on readings and not the lecture so you better memorize every little detail of those 100 pages.   Do not recommend
He is awesome! I actually enjoy his class a lot! Just remember to memorize the years of Chinese dynasties and Japanese periods  as well as the details of certain concepts for the exams!! :D

It's an easy class. Just study the vocab list and know the basics of each religion for the midterm and final. The reflection and essay are really good for boosting your marks. You also have to do homework assignments for each tutorial, which can be tedious. The lectures are sometimes boring, but he's a really sweet and passionate prof.
Seems interested in topic but boring lectures. Tests are easy if you study all of the vocabulary words.

Good GPA booster, not the type of course I wanted to take, nor do I necessarily feel like I've learned a lot from the course.  A lot of unnecessary worksheets but worth taking if you need the grade bump. Really nice prof, if you're interested in the humanities it would likely be more appealing.
Michael is amazingI will definitely choose his other courses......

He's an amazing lecturer. I feel like I learned so much from this course. There are quite a bit of writing assignments, but they are short ones and he's quite lenient with his marking. Attendance does matter. The midterm and final were straightforward. Take this course if you're interested.

don't trust people when they say that this class is easy. I am a chinese born student and trust me is it not easy. It is all based on reading and the questions of the mc and its hard. the final was mc and short answer. the short answer were easy but the mc, oh boy i'm pretty sure i guess on 80% of it.

Newton offers an easy course, full of nothing. The material is quite simple, yet during lectures he goes a methodical pace. He skims his own notes which he reads off his printed powerpoint presentations, yet somehow he will end up behind. Attendance is mandatory for grade, exam like quizzes, and assignmentspapers that are approx 750 words each.

Best prof I've had in all my years at SFU. He loves what he teaches and makes it interesting for students. Professor Newton is very approachable and kind. He doesn't make the course too demanding. Reading the textbook wasnt necessary, only thing on the MT from the book was one single MC question, but you'll need the course reader for assignments.
easy credit
He's pretty easy going, i enjoyed this class. Exams are easy, mc and short answers. Homeworks are easy too, great GPA booster :)
Never liked history class, but I really enjoyed this one!

Super nice guy, I enjoyed attending his lectures, though they were 3 hours long. Assignments are frequent, but really easy, ie. response papers and map exercises. He does his best to keep the class engaged, shows movies + video clips and tell stories and cracks jokes in class.
A lot of work for 10% participation mark, including weekly reading assignments and a boring field trip. Exams are pretty fair.
Semi interesting course. Focuses too much on eastern religions. Tests were fair, but the textbook sucked (it assumed you were an idiot at times, very condescending).

One of the worst classes I've ever taken. Nice enough person and tries to make things interesting, but his exams weren't really fair (said to study one thing, tested on another) and the amount of work and reading required was excessive for a first year course. Lots of make work assignments (like reading q's for no marks).

I had two classes with him and he is the best prof ever. He is knowledgable,kind, nice and helpful. He offers differnt kinds of activities such as, group presentatin, exams, papers and group discussions to make his class very interesting. 3~4 hrs of his class went by very fast. His marking is fair but need to study hard for his exams!

This class was fascinating and Newton managed to present good information and break up the four hours with videos, group discussions and discussion so that it didn't feel as if it was too stretched. Be ready to work though - he gives a lot of small assignments. Our end of term was two papers, an exam, and a presentation.

Really nice prof, definitely improved, even though he was already great! He's less monotone (he was "too zen" his calm voice can put you to sleep) and his classes are even more enjoyable than before. He's showing his sense of humour more and more making the lectures more interesting. There's TOO many assignments he should lose a quiz and paper.

You can tell just by looking at him that he's a good guy. Don't bother buying the textbooks, you won't need it. I have to say though, this prof is super boring. However, the class is extremely easy, just try a bit(like really little) and you'll get an A. I didn't even try and bombed my midterm and still got a B+. Assignments are super easy as well.

he really cares about his students and is really patient to listen our voice. the mt questions are all mc, and the final are mc and t/f, both of which are strongly realted to his notes and what he said during the lecs, so i recommend u to attend the classes although only around 20~40 ppl appeared~

calm and mellow. Unfortunately extremely forgettable also; nothing in lecture really sticks because of it. Doesn't make material particularly interesting--on the whole, his classes are relatively monotonous.

REALLY ZEN! Nice and easy-going. I loved the paper I wrote and this was a great course. The midterm was okay but the final was pretty tough, I'm pretty sure I dropped a letter grade because of it! Study for the final!

He's so calm...so patient. Really nice, answers all the questions thoroughly. i thought the amount of work needed was a bit much for a 1st year course. The project didn't worth much but took a lot of work. Tests weren't that good, asked about things i didn't think were important, like tiny details, nothing on major/central concepts/beliefs

I liked this class~Instructor Newton had made this class very interesting and he made everything clear! He is very patient with his students and he tries very hard to make class environment fun my telling funny stories :)

His Introduction to Religious Studies class inspired me to major in humanities (even though I was more than half way through a degree in psyc). A fabulous and inspiring teacher who helped make the most important decision in my university career.

Love this class!!! Super nice guy.  He's very passionate about what he teaches and he tells funny stories. Interesting lectures.  Very clear on what he expects on the exams.  Marking is extremely fair and the exams are fair.  It's enjoyable to listen to him in lecture because he has a calm voice.  Always available for help.  Good class!!!

AMAZING teacher! The best I've ever had through my 3 years in post-secondary. Very Zen when he teaches, very interesting. Lectures are very focused, knows SO much about Japan and Buddhism and manages to handle it all perfectly. Very kind, surprisingly funny and a great prof. TAKE HIS COURSES!
great guy, very nice, easy marker, did well in his class. hes a buddhist preist and he sooo zen when he teachs, easy class (religious studies) highly recommeded

Also took ASC 201. Prof. Newton seemed to care more about Buddhism than Japanese Culture, and the former was much easier than the latter. He is readily available for students and easy-going during class. He also allows late assignments (I've learned from classmates). He is approachable and kind, but TAKE NOTES! He tests on his own lectures.

Michael is very fair teacher. I found he provided a lot of insight and his personal stories helped to relate the material. The class was very interesting and gave me many new insights into Japanese culture and history. Final, 2 Midterms, paper, movie review, tut. presentation reqd effort but were useful.
